Ad Text:
Role: Senior Software Engineer - Backend

Company: Beonic

Location: Sydney NSW (Hybrid)

Salary: AUD160,000 + Super




Scala & Java 21 platform processing 1B+ journeys/month across global airports and retail venues
Build the data pipelines powering ML-driven crowd flow intelligence - real AI, real scale
Senior voice in architectural direction with a team that values engineering craft




Help us teach physical spaces to think.

At Beonic, we’re digitising the physical world. Our platform processes over 1 billion journeys per month across 11,000+ venues globally, from major international airports to large-scale retail destinations, turning raw sensor data into real-time operational insights.

We’re looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to take meaningful ownership of the systems that make this possible. You’ll be shaping architecture, raising the technical bar across the team, and solving genuinely hard problems at scale.




The Opportunity

You’ll be a senior technical voice in our core engineering team, designing and evolving the server-side architecture that ingests and processes massive streams of IoT data. We’re moving beyond data collection into predictive intelligence: using AI and ML to model crowd dynamics, optimise traffic flow, and surface real-time operational decisions for some of the world’s busiest venues.

Our core platform is Scala and we’re intentionally expanding our use of Java 21 for newer services, so you’ll be working across both and will have genuine input into where and how we draw that line.

Reporting to the VP of Engineering, you’ll have the scope to influence how we build, not just what we build.




What You’ll Achieve

Technical Leadership: Drive architectural decisions and set the standard for code quality, system design, and engineering practices across the team.

Engineering at Scale: Own and optimise high-throughput, low-latency services processing billions of data points from IoT deployments worldwide.

Mentorship: Help mid-level and junior engineers level up through code review, pairing, and honest technical feedback.

End-to-End Ownership: Take features from whiteboard to production, working closely with Product and Platform teams to ship things that actually hold up.




Key Requirements

Deep expertise in Java (we use 21) or Scala
Experience building and scaling distributed systems (ideally using Kafka or similar)
5+ years of commercial software development experience
Proven ability to lead technical work and mentor engineers
Excellent communication skills - you can talk to engineers and non-engineers alike
Solid understanding of development processes, release and support lifecycles
Passionate about software engineering best practices
Able to troubleshoot and resolve production issues under pressure
Knowledge of security best practices for cloud and containerised environments
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience
Full working rights in Australia is a must




Nice to Haves

Cloud experience with AWS, Azure, or GCP
Familiarity with functional programming concepts (a bonus given our Scala codebase)
Hands-on experience with MongoDB, SQL, Elasticsearch
Containerisation experience (Docker, Kubernetes)
Exposure to infrastructure-as-code (Terraform)




Responsibilities

Design, implement, review, test, and deploy features and fixes
Provide support and maintenance for existing production systems
Ensure systems are robust, highly available, monitored, self-healing, and cost-efficient
Mentor and support the growth of less senior engineers on the team
Work closely with Product, Platform and QA to deliver a high-quality customer experience




Diversity and Inclusion

Beonic values the diversity of the people it hires and serves. We want to bring out the best in our global team members by creating a sense of belonging, being a place where they can grow, and ensuring everyone feels valued, heard, and supported.




Perks and Benefits

Hybrid-first: 1–2 days in Surry Hills
Your birthday month = a day off
Dedicated budget for growth and development
Novated lease salary sacrifice
Modern workspace in Surry Hills
Parental leave (after 12 months): 3 months full pay for primary carers, 1 month full pay for partners




Please note: if you are offered a role, background checks may be required, including police, employment, and qualification checks.

Job Title: AI Platform Engineering Manager - AWS | Python | Kubernetes
Ad Text:
About the company:

We are a Market Leading ASX Listed FinTech, we are profitable and we are in a significant growth phase. As part of this growth we are significantly investing in our technology and platforms, which includes integrating AI solutions across the business.

About the Role

We’re looking for a hands on Engineering Manager who can lead talented engineers building AI systems that actually make it to production.

You’ll shape the AI platform, guide technical direction, and roll out solutions that improve efficiency, automation, and user experience across the organisation.

If you love turning vague ideas into rock‑solid AI workflows and you thrive in fast delivery environments, this role is for you.

What You’ll Do
Lead, coach, and grow a high‑performing AI engineering team
Architect and run AI infrastructure (Python, Kubernetes, AWS, Terraform)
Deliver end‑to‑end AI features: discovery > design > build > deploy
Integrate AI components into real products and platforms
Build workflows using emerging tools like Langflow, Bedrock and agent frameworks
Partner with SRE/Platform to keep systems scalable, resilient, and fast
Drive a roadmap that balances innovation with strong engineering foundations
Build a team of innovative AI Engineers
What You Bring
At least 2+ years leading engineers and 1+ years in AI
Strong Python and cloud engineering experience (AWS prefered)
Hands‑on background shipping AI solutions (LLMs, RAG, agents, orchestration)
Background in or strong understanding of Cloud & Platform Engineering
Comfortable designing systems from scratch and owning outcomes
Experience working across multidisciplinary teams
Bonus: Working knowledge of ML concepts
Why This Role Stands Out

You’ll shape the internal AI platform, build scalable systems on modern infrastructure, and lead and grow a team that’s empowered to experiment, move fast, and deliver.

If you want to build real AI products - not endless prototypes - this is your role!
 
Apply below or email your CV directly to josh.kitchin@profusiongroup.com

Join Us at a Defining Moment

At Intelligen, we’re building something different. Over the past three years, we’ve evolved from a bold startup to a trusted partner for some of Australia’s most complex data transformations. We’ve helped organisations move from legacy to modern platforms, embedded governance into decision-making, and brought AI into the hands of the business - responsibly and at speed.

As we continue to grow, we’re looking for senior leaders who can operate at the intersection of advanced analytics, AI and data architecture. This is a role for someone who doesn’t just design technical solutions, but helps clients navigate ambiguity, shape strategy, and build secure, scalable capability that delivers measurable business value.

We’re seeking a Principal Data Consultant to lead high-impact engagements where data, AI, and security architecture converge - bringing trusted advisory capability, deep technical expertise, and delivery leadership to some of the most important transformation programs in market.

About the Role

We are seeking a Principal Data Consultant who can design, deliver, and scale advanced analytics and AI solutions in complex enterprise environments.

This role combines deep technical expertise with strategic advisory capability. You will lead high-impact engagements where data, AI, and architecture intersect - helping organisations translate ambitious goals into secure, production-grade systems that drive real outcomes.

You will operate as both architect and trusted advisor, guiding executive stakeholders through AI adoption while ensuring solutions align to secure-by-design principles, responsible AI practices, and modern data governance requirements.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in complexity, can lead multidisciplinary teams across data engineering, data science, and platform security, and is motivated by solving important client problems with clarity, rigor, and commercial focus.

What You’ll Do

Partner with senior executives to identify high-value opportunities for advanced analytics and AI

Translate strategic business objectives into scalable, secure data and AI architectures

Guide organisations through AI adoption while managing governance, regulatory, risk, and compliance considerations

Architect end-to-end analytics and AI platforms, including data pipelines, machine learning lifecycle, real-time analytics, MLOps, and AI observability

Lead the implementation of secure data and AI systems across modern cloud environments such as AWS, Azure, and GCP

Embed security and privacy into architecture and delivery decisions from the outset

Design systems that are resilient to adversarial risk, model misuse, and operational failure

Lead complex transformation engagements from strategy through execution

Mentor consultants, engineers, and client teams across multidisciplinary delivery environments

Translate technical complexity into clear decision frameworks for executives and senior stakeholders

Support the growth of Intelligen’s advanced analytics and AI capability through thought leadership, client engagement, and delivery excellence

What We’re Looking For

10+ years’ experience delivering advanced analytics, AI, or data transformation solutions in enterprise environments

Proven experience designing and implementing production-grade AI solutions.

Strong expertise across modern data platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, or similar ecosystems

Deep understanding of cloud-native architecture across AWS, Azure, and/or GCP

Demonstrated leadership in large consulting, advisory, or transformation engagements

Strong ability to work with both executive stakeholders and highly technical teams

Excellent communication skills, with the ability to simplify complexity and guide decision-making at senior levels

A mindset grounded in strategic thinking, delivery leadership, and measurable value creation

Nice to Have

Experience implementing MLOps, AI observability, data governance, and AI risk management frameworks

Core Principles for This Role

At Intelligen, we evaluate leaders not only on what they deliver, but how they think, operate, and create impact. For this role, we are especially looking for evidence of the following:

Judgment Under Ambiguity The ability to make strong decisions with incomplete information, navigate uncertainty, and create direction in complex environments.

Secure-by-Design Thinking A consistent approach to embedding security, governance, privacy, and risk into architecture and delivery decisions from day one.

Value Creation A focus on measurable business outcomes, ensuring strategy and technology choices translate into meaningful impact.

Trajectory Evidence that the candidate can already operate one level above the role, with the leadership maturity and strategic capability to scale with the business and our clients.

Systems Thinking The ability to connect strategy, data, AI, operations, and security into coherent solutions that work in practice - not just in theory.

Why Intelligen?

We’re not just delivering AI and data projects.. we’re humanising them. That means we care deeply about the how, not just the what. We value curiosity, creativity, and a willingness to challenge the status quo. We look for people who are driven to build a business, get curious, and offer opinions and ideas.

You’ll join a team that’s smart, kind, and ambitious. You’ll have real influence - in your projects, your practice, and the broader business. And as we grow, so will you.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:




Senior Data Engineer | Data & Analytics | Sydney, AUS

Work on cutting-edge enterprise data solutions using Databricks, Azure Data Factory, DBT, and Python.

Collaborate with a high-performing, agile team to deliver impactful insights across Deloitte.

Continuous professional growth through mentoring, training, and exposure to emerging data engineering technologies.

Deloitte Technology is seeking a skilled Senior Data Engineer to join our Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) team in Sydney. This is a permanent role where you'll play a key part in designing, implementing, and optimising scalable data pipelines and workflows that power Deloitte's data-driven initiatives. You'll have the opportunity to work closely with EDW pod leads and the Senior Data Engineering Manager, contributing to high-impact projects across the business.

What will your typical day look like?

Day to day you will:

Develop and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks and Azure Data Factory.

Transform raw data into actionable insights using Data Build Tools (DBT) and Python.

Build and maintain data integrations across systems using APIs.

Monitor and optimise workflows for performance and cost-efficiency.

Lead project delivery pods, mentor junior team members, and conduct code reviews.

Collaborate with data modelers, analysts, DBAs, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to deliver high-quality solutions.

Document processes, workflows, and system architecture to maintain clarity and knowledge sharing.

About the team

Deloitte Technology's EDW team powers enterprise-wide data initiatives, enabling business users to make informed, data-driven decisions. You'll join a collaborative and high-performing team working in an agile environment, tackling complex data challenges and continuously improving the EDW platform.

Enough about us, let's talk about you

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

5+ years of experience in data engineering, with expertise in Databricks, SQL, Python, Azure Data Factory, and DBT.

Experience designing, building, and optimising data pipelines and workflows.

Hands-on experience with API-based data integrations and version control systems (Azure DevOps, GitHub).

Strong understanding of data architecture patterns and cloud data storage solutions.

Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and adaptability.

Collaborative mindset with strong communication and stakeholder management skills.

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. Advanced degrees or relevant certifications in Azure or Databricks are a plus.

Desirable: Knowledge of Azure Fabric and emerging data engineering technologies.

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating, and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - up, into new areas, or across the globe.

We embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and cultivate inclusion, making our work environment a space where everyone belongs.

We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.

We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave, and a return-to-work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!


#LI-Hybrid

We are looking for two Python-savvy developers who specialize in FastAPI and AWS to build robust, scalable integration points. You'll be working within a high-visibility technical squad to ensure our data flows seamlessly across the enterprise, bridging the gap between our evaluation and technical systems.


What You'll Do

Architect Connections: Design and implement high-performance APIs using FastAPI within an AWS ecosystem.
Stream Data: Work within a Kafka environment to manage real-time data streaming and event-driven architectures.
Bridge Systems: Apply deep knowledge of integration principles to connect disparate platforms, ensuring data integrity and low latency.
Collaborate & Pivot: Operate within a "Squad" model, working autonomously on specific tasks while remaining tightly aligned with functional, business, and technical leads.
Deployment & Debugging: Get your "hands dirty" in the dev environment, troubleshooting complex integration flows and optimizing performance.

Must-Haves

Python Specialist: Expert-level experience with FastAPI.
Cloud Native: Strong background in AWS environments (Lambda, S3, EC2, etc.).
Message Brokering: Hands-on experience working within Kafka for streaming and messaging.
Integration Logic: A deep understanding of RESTful services, webhooks, and asynchronous processing.
Agile Mindset: The ability to work independently but thrive in a collaborative "squad" setting.
Communication: Clear, concise technical communication for reporting into the wider project team.

Nice-to-Haves

Data Orchestration: Experience with Airflow.
Testing/Documentation: Proficiency with Postman.
Platform Knowledge: Familiarity with Snowflake or Salesforce (specifically MuleSoft Gateway).

The Team & Environment
You will be part of a multi-disciplinary technical squad. This is a fast-paced environment where you'll have the support of business and functional leads, but the autonomy to own your technical domain and drive integration excellence.

We're looking for a senior Performance Architect to help us take our platforms to the next level. Working closely with our Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) team, you'll define what "high performance" looks like and drive initiatives that make our software faster, more scalable, and more reliable for our customers.

This is a senior, influential role where you'll shape engineering culture around performance and resilience. You'll set standards, KPIs, and SLOs, guide teams on best practices, and help ensure our enterprise software runs seamlessly at scale. While strategic and advisory, there's also room to get hands-on with performance tuning, load testing, and optimisation if that's your strength.


What you'll be doing

Define and champion performance, scalability, and reliability standards across engineering teams
Partner with SRE, architecture, platform, and delivery teams to embed resilience in every system
Set and monitor KPIs, SLOs, error budgets, and benchmarks, driving accountability across teams
Lead reviews of infrastructure, application code, and data layers to identify performance improvements
Analyse faults and system behaviours to pinpoint systemic issues
Prioritise and deliver performance and scalability optimisation initiatives
Guide teams in load testing, capacity planning, and fault testing
Build dashboards and reporting to give clear visibility into system health and performance
Advise engineering leaders on risks, trade-offs, and performance investment decisions
Promote continuous improvement in engineering practices and culture


Your Talents…

8+ years' experience with large-scale, distributed, or enterprise software systems
Strong knowledge of performance, scalability, and reliability across infrastructure, code, and databases
Experience working with SRE, platform, or operations teams in high-availability environments
Proven track record defining and driving KPIs, SLOs, and error budgets at scale
Hands-on experience with performance analysis, profiling, load testing, and fault investigation
Broad technical skill set, comfortable across architecture, code, data, and infrastructure
Strong communication and influencing skills
Pragmatic approach, balancing ideal outcomes with real-world constraints
Enterprise software or SaaS experience


Benefits…

Competitive remuneration package
Industry leading employee share plan
Amazing Culture: 8 x Australian Business Awards "Employer of Choice" winner
We love our team events & celebrate diversity
Flexible hours: we are family-friendly and value life outside of work
Free gym membership in your region (or, if you are located at HQ, we have a new state of the art Gym onsite, exclusive and available 24/7).
Additional 2.5 days of leave per year dedicated to volunteering at a charity of your choice
Free breakfast and coffee on-site
Defined career framework - know what options you have in your career path and how you can get there


Who we are…

TechnologyOne (ASX: TNE) is Australia's largest enterprise software company and one of the top 100 ASX-listed companies, with offices in six countries. Our global SaaS ERP platform powers over 1,200 organisations, helping them focus on their business, not technology.

For more than 36 years, we've been evolving enterprise software to adapt to new technologies, making life simple for our customers wherever they are.

Drive data-led growth. Champion AI and shape the future of banking

We are seeking a Data and AI Lead to play a pivotal role in strengthening our data and capability and accelerating the responsible adoption of AI across the organisation. This is a strategic hands-on role for someone who can bridge deep technical ability with business impact - championing data governance, scalable architecture, and AI-driven innovation that deliver real outcomes for our members and our people.

About the role

As the Data and AI Lead, you will lead enterprise-wide Data and AI initiatives that improve business performance, competitiveness and decision making. You will work closely with stakeholders across the Bank to embed high quality data practices and AI solutions into core business processes, while ensuring strong governance, security, and regulatory compliance.

Key Responsibilities

In this role, you will:

Champion and lead data initiatives that enhance business performance and strengthen the Bank’s market position.       
Build and implement strong data management practices, including data cleansing, normalisation, and enrichment to support dashboards, reporting, and AI/ML tools.
Establish and maintain enterprise-wide governance frameworks, including data quality standards, metadata management and stewardship practices.
Drive AI adoption across the organisation by engaging stakeholders, running workshops and clearly communicating the value of AI to non-technical teams.
Design and oversee scalable secure data architectures and pipelines that support analytics and AI use cases.
Ensure compliance with data privacy, security and regulatory requirements through robust controls and monitoring.
Select and implement fit for purpose tools, platforms, and frameworks that balance cost, scalability and performance.
Collaborate with cross functional and external partners (including product, engineering and operations) to embed AI into business and customer facing solutions.
Lead data and AI projects end to end, from scoping and planning through to engineering, delivery and outcomes.

About you:

You are a forward-thinking data professional who:

Bring strong experience in data platforms and analytics.
Experience building/engineering data models and sourcing data from various sources.
Experience with Databricks, Snowflake and PowerBI.
Understanding of Financial Data or Banking Products/Services desirable.
Can translate complex technical concepts into clear, compelling business value.
Has a strong understanding of data governance, privacy and operating in regulated environments..
Is curious commercially minded, and passionate about using Data and AI responsibly.

Why join MoveBank?

At MoveBank, we are building a digital first, member owned bank where Digital, Data and AI are key enablers of growth, efficiency, and better member experiences. You will have the opportunity to shape foundational capabilities, influence strategy, and deliver meaningful outcomes in an organisation that values integrity, collaboration, and integrity.

Company Description

We are proud winners of the Best Place to Work awards in 2025 and 2026. Aviato Consulting, founded by ex-Googlers, specializes in Google Cloud solutions. We combine deep technical expertise with the open, fun engineering culture of Google minus the bureaucracy.

What's in it for you?

Build the "Next" Tech: Working heavily on AI, you will be using the newest models, to build agents to solve our customers problems.

Elite Engineering Culture: Work alongside ex-Googlers and cloud experts. We prioritize clean code, high velocity shipping, and technical excellence over politics.

Pure GCP Playground: You get full access to the Vertex AI ecosystem (Gemini 3 Pro/Flash, Claude, Vector Search, Agent Engine) to build solutions.

Verified Happiness: Join a team objectively recognized as a "Best Place to Work" for two years running. We take culture as seriously as we take our architecture.

ESOP: Share in our success with our employee option plan

Role Description

Agentic Architecture: Design scalable, event-driven architectures for single and multi-agent systems using Google’s Gemini models.

Advanced Reasoning: Implement sophisticated prompting strategies (ReAct, Chain of Thought) and evaluation frameworks to ensure agent reliability.

Tooling & Integration: Equip agents with executable tools by integrating internal databases (BigQuery, Cloud SQL) and APIs via Apigee or Cloud Functions.

Data Grounding: Engineer advanced RAG pipelines using Vertex AI Search and Vector Search to provide agents with real-time, factual context.

Orchestration: Manage agent state, planning, and task execution using frameworks like A2A or MCP.

Production Deployment: Containerize and deploy agentic applications as scalable microservices using Agent Engine or Cloud Run.

What You'll Bring to the Table:

Proven Google Cloud Expertise: 2+ years of hands-on experience building and implementing solutions on Google Cloud Platform.

A Google Cloud Certification is a must-have within 1 month of hiring, and advantageous to landing the role.

Candidates must hold a valid work visa or be eligible to work in Australia.

Experience with Agent Development Kit, A2A, Vertex AI

Bonus Points:

Experience with data architecture, big data environments, and ETL pipelines.

Langraph/Langchain

Evaluation frameworks

Our Core Values:

Radical Transparency: We build trust by speaking clearly and listening carefully. We ensure our clients, partners (Google) and teammates know what is happening and feel heard. We choose clarity over comfort every time.

We win and lose as a team. We take pride in our own work, but we always step in to help a teammate because we are all responsible for the final result.

Excellence & Growth: We reject "good enough" we do excellent work, We help each other get better every day. We give kind and helpful feedback, we teach what we know, and we treat mistakes as a chance for the whole team to learn.

Customer Value: We act like partners, not just consultants. When a problem happens, we don't complain, we find the best solution and create value for our clients aiming to deliver a 10x return on our client's trust by solving the hard problems before we are asked to.

📢 Position: Data Engineer (Cyber)
✅ Eligibility: Open to candidates with valid Australian work rights
🕒 Contract: Initial 3 months of contract with high likelihood of extension

📍 Location: Northwest Sydney, NSW
🏠 Hybrid Work Model: Minimum 2 days onsite per week

👇About the Role:

As a Data Engineer (Cyber), you will work closely with senior leads across Cyber Engineering, SecOps, and Data Platform to leverage existing patterns and frameworks to:

Onboard new data sources (logs, alerts, context) into the platform.
Remediate and uplift legacy code for reliability, performance, and readability.
Develop and enhance data models (via DBT) to codify business rules and cyber logic.
Implement validation tests and documentation to maintain data trust.
Troubleshoot pipeline and cloud infra issues impacting ingestion, modeling, and access.
Collaborate with stakeholders to manage expectations and prioritize work in an agile cadence.
Continuously improve our cyber data ecosystem through automation, CI/CD, and standards.

👇Key Responsibilities:

Write ingestion configurations (connectors, schemas, mappings).
Create staging tables/views and dbt source definitions.
Implement incremental loading patterns and late‑arriving data handling.
Enforce PII/security controls (masking, hashing, row/column access).
Add data quality tests (generic/custom), documentation and lineage/exposures.
Contribute to Git‑based workflows (branches, PRs, reviews) and CI/CD for dbt.
Support backfills, replays, and validation tooling for operational resilience.

👇Essential Skills:

SQL (strong): joins, window functions, dedupe patterns, incremental modeling, handling semi‑structured logs.
Git (strong): branching, PRs, code reviews, semantic commits.
Python: light utilities for schema inference, API pulls (when ingestion isn’t fully managed), validation/backfills/replay tooling.
JSON/YAML: config‑driven pipelines and declarative patterns.
Cyber domain literacy: SIEM concepts, MITRE ATT&CK understanding, clear grasp of threats vs. vulnerabilities, SOC data needs, common telemetry (EDR, identity, network, cloud).
Ways of working: Agile/sprint delivery, stakeholder communication, concise documentation.

👇Preferred Skills / Experience:

Cloud & Warehouse: GCP BigQuery (preferred) and/or Snowflake/Databricks; cloud storage patterns.
DBT (Data Build Tools: sources, staging/core models, incremental models, macros/Jinja (intermediate+), tests (generic + custom), snapshots (nice), exposures/docs (nice).
DevOps/SecOps: CI/CD pipelines, IaC (e.g., Terraform), secrets mgmt, policy‑as‑code, scan gates.
Comfort with log formats (JSON/CSV/syslog/parquet), time normalization, IP/UUID parsing; understands event‑time vs ingest‑time, correlation keys, identity stitching
SIEM: hands‑on with any major SIEM (e.g., Chronicle/Splunk/Sentinel/QRadar) is a plus.

 

👇About You:

You’re someone who takes initiative, enjoys collaborative problem-solving, and is comfortable navigating large-scale cyber data ecosystems. You ask the right questions, learn quickly, and adapt to evolving priorities.
If this sounds like you, we encourage you to apply and join a team making meaningful impact in the cyber data space.

This is an exciting opportunity to join a dynamic team at the forefront of retail cybersecurity, working within a collaborative and innovative environment.





Join our growing global team and accelerate your career with us. Apply today.

For more information about this or other opportunities, please contact Aasifa Ruhani. Please quote our job reference number 665606.

About us

NTT Ltd. is a leading global technology services company. Working with organizations around the world, we achieve business outcomes through intelligent technology solutions. For us, intelligent means data driven, connected, digital and secure. As a global ICT provider, we employ more than 40,000 people in a diverse and dynamic workplace that spans 57 countries, trading in 73 countries and delivering services in over 200 countries and regions

NTT is proud to be an equal opportunity employer with a global culture that embraces diversity. We are committed to providing an environment free of unfair discrimination and harassment. We do not discriminate based on age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, pregnancy, marital status, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, veteran status, or other protected category.

PLC Programmer / Systems Integrator

Australian Refrigeration Services (ARS) is seeking an experienced PLC Programmer / Systems Integrator to support the design, implementation, and ongoing support of industrial control systems across our client sites throughout NSW.

This role plays a key part in strengthening ARS’s automation capability, with a strong focus on industrial refrigeration, PLC control systems, SCADA platforms, and remote monitoring solutions.

Working closely with the Technical Director and project teams, you will contribute to delivering reliable, efficient, and innovative automation systems across service and project environments.

Key Responsibilities

Your duties will include, but are not limited to:

Design, develop, modify, and maintain PLC, HMI, and SCADA systems for ARS projects and service clients.

Program and support control systems using Allen-Bradley PLC platforms and Ignition SCADA systems.

Integrate control systems with industrial refrigeration, mechanical, and electrical plant equipment.

Perform commissioning, testing, fault-finding, and optimisation of control systems both on-site and remotely.

Develop and maintain control documentation, including I/O lists, network architecture, and system backups.

Provide technical support and troubleshooting to ARS technicians, supervisors, and clients.

Assist in developing standardised control philosophies, templates, and programming structures.

Ensure systems comply with relevant standards, safety requirements, and ARS best-practice guidelines.

Maintain strong working relationships with clients, equipment manufacturers, and technology suppliers.

Support remote monitoring platforms, alarm management, and after-hours support when required.

Contribute to the continuous improvement of ARS automation and control capabilities.

In conjunction with the Technical Director, you will:

Support the delivery of automation and control systems across NSW service and project operations.

Work closely with refrigeration technicians and project teams to ensure seamless system integration.

Assist in training technicians and apprentices on control system interfaces and diagnostics.

Help improve reliability, efficiency, and performance across client sites through smarter automation.

Participate in technical reviews, audits, and system upgrades.

About You

To succeed in this role, you will be a self-motivated and technically capable professional who is confident working independently and solving complex technical problems.

You will demonstrate:

Proven experience with Allen-Bradley PLC programming

Hands-on experience with Ignition SCADA systems (essential)

Experience with Siemens, Citect, or Wonderware will be highly regarded

Strong troubleshooting and system integration skills

The ability to work independently and manage tasks across multiple sites

A practical, solutions-focused approach

Excellent communication and documentation skills

Additional Requirements

Current Class C Driver Licence

Willingness to travel to regional and interstate locations when required

Ability to work independently for extended periods

A professional and mature approach to client engagement

Why Join ARS?

This is an opportunity to play a key role in the growth of ARS’s automation and systems integration capability while working on specialised industrial refrigeration and automation projects across NSW.

You will have the opportunity to apply your expertise, influence control standards, and help shape the next phase of ARS’s automation offering.

If you are a motivated PLC programmer or systems integrator looking to work on challenging industrial systems within a supportive and technically focused team, we would welcome your application.

Please email our Technical Director Andrew Hall Andrew.hall@ausrefrig.com.au with your resume and cover letter.

Job Description

About Learning Online Group 
Learning Online Group (LOG) is a fast-moving online education company operating across Australia, Canada, the UK, and New Zealand. With 115 staff and $6M in annual paid media spend, we run a portfolio of education brands spanning beauty, photography, animal care, and more. We're a business that backs its people. We take deliberate bets on candidates who show the right instincts and drive — even if their CV doesn't tick every box — and we give them the room to grow into the role and beyond it. A number of our senior team members joined us early in their careers and have grown with the business. We're proud of that, and we're looking to repeat it.
We invest in learning. If there are tools, courses, or experiences that make you better at your job, we want to know about them. We'd rather have someone who is genuinely curious and developing fast than someone who already knows everything and stops there. AI is not a future priority for us — it's something we're actively building into our operations right now. This role exists because the work is real and growing, and we want the right person to own it.

The Role 
This is a full-time, hands-on role responsible for implementing AI-powered systems across Learning Online Group. You'll design, build, and maintain automations, integrations, internal tools, and AI workflows that reduce manual work, improve how our sales and marketing teams perform, and give leadership better visibility into what's happening across the business.  You'll work directly with the CEO and department heads to identify high-value problems, then build solutions — working across our stack of Salesforce, Vonage, Customer.io, and WordPress, connected through Make.com and increasingly through AI agents and tools like Claude Code and Cowork.  This is a rare opportunity for an early-career candidate to take genuine ownership of a function, rather than supporting someone else's. You'll shape how AI is used inside a 115-person business, with real budget and real stakeholders.

What You'll Do 

Map and prioritise AI and automation opportunities across the business in partnership with the CEO
Build and maintain Make.com workflows connecting our core systems (Salesforce, Vonage, Customer.io, WordPress)
Develop and manage internal dashboards that surface key performance data to sales, marketing, and operations teams
Implement AI agents and tools using platforms like Claude Code and Cowork to automate complex, multi-step internal processes
Document what you build so that it can be maintained, improved, and handed off as the function grows
Stay across the fast-moving AI tooling landscape and proactively identify new capabilities worth evaluating
Work with department heads to understand workflows and translate business problems into system solutions
 
What We're Looking For:

Must-have

Hands-on experience with automation platforms — Make.com, Zapier, n8n, or similar
Comfort working across APIs and connecting SaaS systems without direct engineering support
Strong problem-solving instincts — you can take a vague business problem and figure out how to build a solution
A portfolio or examples of automations, integrations, or tools you've built (personal projects count)
Excellent communication — you'll work directly with non-technical stakeholders and need to translate between business need and system capability
Strong candidates will also have

Basic coding ability (Python, JavaScript, or similar) — not required, but significantly expands what you can build
Experience working with AI tools like Claude, GPT, or similar APIs
Familiarity with CRM systems, particularly Salesforce
Exposure to tools like Claude Code, Cowork, or AI agent frameworks
A degree or coursework in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or a related field
 
Who You Are 

Self-directed — you don't need a detailed brief to get started, you can figure out what needs doing
Motivated by impact — you want to see what you build actually change how a business operates
Comfortable with ambiguity — this is a new function and you'll be helping define it
Fast learner — the tooling moves quickly and you enjoy keeping up
Commercially minded — you think about whether the thing you're building is actually worth the time it takes to build it
 
What We Offer 

Real scope and autonomy from day one — you'll own the AI build-out, not assist someone else's
Direct access to the CEO and leadership team
A fast-moving environment where your work ships quickly and has visible impact
Exposure to a $6M+ paid media operation and multi-market business
Competitive salary ($80,000 – $110,000 AUD depending on experience)
Hybrid working from Melbourne
 
How to Apply 
Send your CV and a brief note (no more than a page) describing the most complex automation or system you've built — what the problem was, how you approached it, and what the outcome was. We don't care about formatting. We care about thinking and evidence of building. Applications without a practical example will not be progressed.

Sydney|Melbourne|Brisbane | Full Time | Hybrid



About the Role

I-MED Radiology Network is seeking a Senior Integrations Lead to drive the design and delivery of mission-critical healthcare integrations that connect hospitals, radiology systems, and clinical workflows.

You will lead the development of integration solutions using HL7, DICOM, and the Rhapsody integration engine, ensuring seamless information flow between internal systems, external healthcare partners, and clinicians. This role also includes mentoring junior engineers and shaping integration standards across the IT team.

What You'll Do


Lead the design, development, and support of enterprise integration solutions connecting RIS, PACS, EMRs, and other hospital systems
Work closely with clinical, operational, and IT stakeholders to gather requirements and deliver reliable solutions
Ensure integrations meet architecture standards, governance, and best practices
Monitor and troubleshoot integration issues, proactively resolving incidents
Mentor and coach junior team members in HL7, DICOM, Rhapsody, and integration best practices
Contribute to continuous improvement of integration processes, tools, and documentation

About You


Degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field
Strong experience with enterprise system integrations
Hands-on experience with HL7 messaging standards
Experience working with Rhapsody Integration Engine or similar middleware platforms
Exposure to medical imaging workflows (DICOM) desirable
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver outcomes

About I-MED: Work that matters

I-MED is a global leader and ANZ's largest, privately owned radiology network. The work we do every day is at the core of providing quality medical imaging services to patients across our 270 clinics. When you work for I-MED you form part of a critical supply chain that supports emergency departments, obstetrics, oncology clinics and we deliver over 6.5 Million patient procedures annually.

Backed by global private equity, today we are a $1.2B business with additional joint venture partnerships, that includes a global leader in Artificial Intelligence technology for Radiology.

Why Join Us

Enjoy exclusive benefits including discounted private health insurance, salary packaging, Fitness Passport access, lifestyle and travel discounts, I-MED services for your family and employee wellbeing support.

At I-MED Radiology Network you'll work on technology that directly supports clinicians and patient care, while being part of a collaborative, innovative, and supportive IT team.

You'll have the opportunity to lead technically complex projects, influence integration standards, and mentor the next generation of healthcare integration engineers.

Pay.com.au is a high-growth fintech redefining B2B payments, helping over 50,000 businesses process billions in payments and earn billions in points through its PayRewards program. They turn everyday business spend into extraordinary value through transfer partners such as Qantas, Virgin Australia and Singapore Airlines.

Founded in Melbourne, the company sits at the intersection of payments, credit and loyalty. Businesses use the platform to pay suppliers, manage expenses and earn rewards.

The company has grown rapidly in recent years, with revenue more than doubling in FY2025. As part of its next stage of growth, Pay.com.au is expanding internationally and preparing for a potential ASX listing.

The Role

We're looking for an AI Adoption Lead to help identify and implement practical ways AI can improve how the business operates.

This is not a product management role, nor is it a pure engineering position. Instead, the focus is on experimentation, rapid prototyping and applying AI to real operational problems across the organisation.

The goal is to find someone who enjoys building and testing things. Someone who is constantly experimenting with new AI tools, thinking about practical use cases, and building lightweight solutions that make teams more effective.

A large part of the role will involve identifying friction in existing workflows, testing whether AI can improve them, building simple MVPs and iterating based on real feedback from teams.

The emphasis is on speed, experimentation and practical outcomes rather than large transformation programs.

What You’ll Be Doing

Identify opportunities where AI can improve internal workflows across operations, marketing, sales and other teams
Rapidly prototype AI-powered tools, automations or workflows
Test ideas quickly and iterate based on feedback from internal teams
Work with engineers and data teams to safely deploy useful solutions
Experiment with emerging AI tools and frameworks
Help build internal momentum and capability around AI adoption
Translate AI capabilities into practical business applications
This role reports directly to a senior leader in the business and will work closely with engineering, data, marketing and operations teams.
Areas Where AI Could Make an Impact

Customer verification and operational workflows that currently involve manual steps
Marketing asset creation and campaign experimentation
Extracting insights from sales conversations and internal communications
Agent-based workflow automation across internal systems
These areas are starting points rather than a fixed roadmap. The expectation is that the person hired will identify additional opportunities as they go.

Technology Environment

The role will interact with a range of internal systems and data sources, including AWS infrastructure, Databricks and other operational platforms used across the business.

You’ll also work closely with engineers, analysts and data scientists who can help support the development and deployment of useful solutions.

What Success Looks Like

In the first three to six months, success will come from building working prototypes and demonstrating clear value for internal teams. Examples might include automating a repetitive operational task, building a tool to extract insights from internal data, or creating an AI workflow to speed up a marketing or sales process.

Over time, the goal is to embed AI into several core workflows across the business and help teams work more efficiently.

About You

This role will suit someone who spends a lot of time experimenting with AI tools and thinking about how to apply them to real problems.

You don’t need to be a deep machine learning specialist, but you should be comfortable working with modern AI tooling.

You must be someone who:

Regularly experiments with new AI tools and models
Enjoy building prototypes, MVPs and small automation tools
Think about how AI can improve real workflows
Are comfortable working independently in a lean environment
Are confident in challenging existing processes
Have experience working with tools such as Python, LLM APIs, automation platforms or modern AI frameworks
Supporting Information

Hybrid role, 3 days in and 2 at home. Office currently in Elsternwick, soon to relocate to Windsor
Salary: $120-150k + super, negotiable for someone exceptional
